A valid compressed key plus empty ciphertext would stringify upstream but throw in C#. | Match TS slicing behavior, or only reject `< 33` if defensive validation is desired. |
codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r3-20260523-prefix-evidence.md:6108:./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r1-20260523.md:5196:| `src/Crypto.cs:827-830` | `ts-source/turnkey.ts:164-180` | SOLANA export output is wrong: C# Base58-encodes only 32 decrypted private-key bytes; upstream derives Ed25519 public key, concatenates 32 private + 32 public bytes, then Base58-encodes 64 bytes. | Derive Ed25519 public key and encode the 64-byte concatenation. |
codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r3-20260523-prefix-evidence.md:6111:./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r1-20260523.md:5203:`tests/Fixtures/` currently contains only `tests/Fixtures/encoding/turnkey-encoding-vectors.json`; no fixture under `tests/Fixtures/` exercises `src/Crypto.cs`. `tests/Fixtures/README.md` lists `crypto/` as planned, but the directory is absent. `tests/CryptoTests.cs` uses inline RFC/NIST/random roundtrip tests and explicitly states positive Turnkey-signed bundle tests are not present.

codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r3-20260523-prefix-evidence.md:6254:./codex-crypto-reviews/Encoding.cs-r1-20260523.md:308:src/turnkey-sdk-csharp.csproj:12:    <Description>Unofficial / community-maintained Turnkey SDK for .NET by Kyuzan Inc. Not affiliated with Turnkey, Inc. 1:1 logical port of @turnkey/crypto v2.8.8, @turnkey/http v3.16.0, @turnkey/api-key-stamper v0.5.0, and @turnkey/encoding v0.6.0 (peak-pinned versions). Provides P-256 ECDSA / HPKE / HKDF / API key stamping primitives for .NET. BouncyCastle 2.5.0 is used only for primitives; HPKE / HKDF / bundle parsing are direct ports of the upstream Turnkey TypeScript logic.</Description>

codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r3-20260523-prefix-evidence.md:6354:./codex-crypto-reviews/Encoding.cs-r1-20260523.md:3740:/bin/zsh -lc "node -e \"function hexToAscii(s){let o=''; for(let i=0;i<s.length;i+=2)o+=String.fromCharCode(parseInt(s.substr(i,2),16)); return Array.from(o).map(c=>c.charCodeAt(0)).join(',');} function b64urlToBase64(input){let b64=input.replace(/-/g,'+').replace(/_/g,'/'); const padLen=(4-(b64.length%4))%4; return b64+'='.repeat(padLen);} function atob2(input){const str=String(input).replace(/["'^A-Za-z0-9+/=]/g,'"''); if(str.length%4===1) throw new Error('InvalidCharacterError'); const keyStr='ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZabcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz0123456789+/='; let output='',buffer=0,bits=0,i=0; while(i<str.length){const ch=str.charAt(i); const index=keyStr.indexOf(ch); if(index<0||index>64){i++; continue;} if(ch==='='){bits=0;} else {buffer=(buffer<<6)|index; bits+=6;} while(bits>=8){bits-=8; output+=String.fromCharCode((buffer>>bits)&0xff);} i++;} return output;} console.log('hex f ->', hexToAscii('f')); console.log('hex zz ->', hexToAscii('zz')); console.log('decode a*GVsbG8 ->', atob2(b64urlToBase64('a*GVsbG8')));\"" in /Users/takeshi/Kyuzan/src/turnkey-sdk-csharp
codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r3-20260523-prefix-evidence.md:6355:./codex-crypto-reviews/Encoding.cs-r1-20260523.md:3747:/bin/zsh -lc 'node -e "function u(hexString,length){const hexRegex=/''^[0-9A-Fa-f]+$/; if(!hexString || hexString.length%2!=0 || !hexRegex.test(hexString)) throw new Error('"'invalid'); const buffer=new Uint8Array(hexString.match(/../g).map(h=>parseInt(h,16))); if("'!length) return buffer; if(hexString.length/2>length) throw new Error('"'too long'); const p=new Uint8Array(length); p.set(buffer,length-buffer.length); return p;} console.log(Array.from(u('01',0)).join(','));\"" in /Users/takeshi/Kyuzan/src/turnkey-sdk-csharp

codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r3-20260523-prefix-evidence.md:8773:| `GetPublicKey(byte[])` (`src/Crypto.cs:344`) | `ts-source/crypto.ts:80` | REVIEWED | Invalid-key validation divergence in E. |
codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r3-20260523-prefix-evidence.md:8800:- C# `byte[]` plus `Array.Copy` replaces `Uint8Array.slice/set/spread`; structural byte movement only.
codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r3-20260523-prefix-evidence.md:8806:- Sync C# methods replace async TS wrappers where no C# async operation is needed; output bytes unchanged.
codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r3-20260523-prefix-evidence.md:8808:- Ed25519 seed-to-public derivation uses BouncyCastle for the SOLANA export branch; intended same 32-byte public key.
codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r3-20260523-prefix-evidence.md:8819:| `src/Crypto.cs:344`, `src/Crypto.cs:364` | `ts-source/crypto.ts:80` | C# accepts non-32-byte private key inputs and does not explicitly reject scalar `0` / `>= n`; noble `p256.getPublicKey` validates exact key size and scalar range. | Add a shared P-256 private-key validator: exactly 32 bytes and scalar in `[1, n - 1]`. |
codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r3-20260523-prefix-evidence.md:8823:- `tests/Fixtures/crypto/turnkey-crypto-vectors.json` is pinned to `@turnkey/crypto@2.8.8` and the same tarball SHA above.

codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r3-20260523.md:14888:./src/turnkey-sdk-csharp.csproj:12:    <Description>Unofficial / community-maintained Turnkey SDK for .NET by Kyuzan Inc. Not affiliated with Turnkey, Inc. 1:1 logical port of @turnkey/crypto v2.8.8, @turnkey/http v3.16.0, @turnkey/api-key-stamper v0.5.0, and @turnkey/encoding v0.6.0 (peak-pinned versions). Provides P-256 ECDSA / HPKE / HKDF / API key stamping primitives for .NET. BouncyCastle 2.5.0 is used only for primitives; HPKE / HKDF / bundle parsing are direct ports of the upstream Turnkey TypeScript logic.</Description>

codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r3-20260523.md:15044:./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r3-20260523.md:588:codex-crypto-reviews/upstream-snapshots/turnkey-crypto-2.8.8/package/README.md:3:This package consolidates some common cryptographic utilities used across our applications, particularly primitives related to keys, encryption, and decryption in a pure JS implementation. For react-native you will need to polyfill our random byte generation by importing [react-native-get-random-values](https://www.npmjs.com/package/react-native-get-random-values)

codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r3-20260523.md:16282:./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:5417:5048:| `DeriveSS` (`src/Crypto.cs:898`) | `deriveSS` (`ts-source/crypto.ts:559`) | REVIEWED | Returns x-coordinate shared secret, padded to 32 bytes. |
codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r3-20260523.md:16296:./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:5431:5086:| `src/Crypto.cs:355` | `ts-source/crypto.ts:358-363` | C# uses `ECKeyPairGenerator`, which samples valid scalars; TS samples 32 random bytes then derives public key. Rare invalid scalar/failure/distribution behavior differs. | If strict parity is required, generate 32 random bytes and derive with the same validation behavior; otherwise document as deliberate hardening. |
codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r3-20260523.md:16299:./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:5434:5089:| `src/Crypto.cs:482-486` | `ts-source/crypto.ts:406-418` | C# rejects non-65-byte or non-`0x04` input; TS slices any input and mutates prefix based on last byte. Valid public keys match, malformed input behavior differs. | Remove extra validation for parity or keep and document stricter validation. |
codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r3-20260523.md:16302:./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:5437:5092:| `src/Crypto.cs:579-582` | `ts-source/crypto.ts:286-299` | C# rejects buffers `<= 33`; TS slices first 33 bytes and allows downstream behavior. A valid compressed key plus empty ciphertext would stringify upstream but throw in C#. | Match TS slicing behavior, or only reject `< 33` if defensive validation is desired. |
codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r3-20260523.md:16308:./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:5443:5098:| `src/Crypto.cs:827-830` | `ts-source/turnkey.ts:164-180` | SOLANA export output is wrong: C# Base58-encodes only 32 decrypted private-key bytes; upstream derives Ed25519 public key, concatenates 32 private + 32 public bytes, then Base58-encodes 64 bytes. | Derive Ed25519 public key and encode the 64-byte concatenation. |
codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r3-20260523.md:16313:./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:5449:5133:| `GetPublicKey(byte[])` (`src/Crypto.cs:321`) | `getPublicKey` (`ts-source/crypto.ts:80`) | REVIEWED | P-256 SEC1 output matches compressed flag. |

codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r3-20260523.md:16394:./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:5565:No fixture under `tests/Fixtures/` exercises `src/Crypto.cs`. The only fixture present is `tests/Fixtures/encoding/turnkey-encoding-vectors.json`, which is for `Encoding.cs`. `tests/Fixtures/README.md` mentions a future `crypto/` fixture directory, but it is not present.
codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r3-20260523.md:16395:./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:5567:Result: fixture gate is not satisfiable as “all fixtures match” for `Crypto.cs`; there are no pinned Node-generated Crypto fixtures to compare. Existing `tests/CryptoTests.cs` uses RFC literals, NIST-style known values, negative cases, and randomized C# roundtrips, not pinned upstream fixture files.

codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r3-20260523.md:16438:./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:5652:No fixture under `tests/Fixtures/` exercises `src/Crypto.cs`. codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r3-20260523.md:16439:./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:5654:Result: fixture gate is not satisfiable as “all fixtures match” for `Crypto.cs`; there are no pinned Node-generated Crypto fixtures to compare. Existing `tests/CryptoTests.cs` uses RFC literals, NIST-style known values, negative cases, and randomized C# roundtrips, not pinned upstream fixture files.

codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r3-20260523.md:17177:./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r1-20260523.md:5086:| `src/Crypto.cs:355` | `ts-source/crypto.ts:358-363` | C# uses `ECKeyPairGenerator`, which samples valid scalars; TS samples 32 random bytes then derives public key. Rare invalid scalar/failure/distribution behavior differs. | If strict parity is required, generate 32 random bytes and derive with the same validation behavior; otherwise document as deliberate hardening. |
codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r3-20260523.md:17180:./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r1-20260523.md:5089:| `src/Crypto.cs:482-486` | `ts-source/crypto.ts:406-418` | C# rejects non-65-byte or non-`0x04` input; TS slices any input and mutates prefix based on last byte. Valid public keys match, malformed input behavior differs. | Remove extra validation for parity or keep and document stricter validation. |
codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r3-20260523.md:17183:./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r1-20260523.md:5092:| `src/Crypto.cs:579-582` | `ts-source/crypto.ts:286-299` | C# rejects buffers `<= 33`; TS slices first 33 bytes and allows downstream behavior. A valid compressed key plus empty ciphertext would stringify upstream but throw in C#. | Match TS slicing behavior, or only reject `< 33` if defensive validation is desired. |
codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r3-20260523.md:17189:./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r1-20260523.md:5098:| `src/Crypto.cs:827-830` | `ts-source/turnkey.ts:164-180` | SOLANA export output is wrong: C# Base58-encodes only 32 decrypted private-key bytes; upstream derives Ed25519 public key, concatenates 32 private + 32 public bytes, then Base58-encodes 64 bytes. | Derive Ed25519 public key and encode the 64-byte concatenation. |
codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r3-20260523.md:17192:./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r1-20260523.md:5105:`tests/Fixtures/` currently contains only `tests/Fixtures/encoding/turnkey-encoding-vectors.json`; no fixture under `tests/Fixtures/` exercises `src/Crypto.cs`. `tests/Fixtures/README.md` lists `crypto/` as planned, but the directory is absent. `tests/CryptoTests.cs` uses inline RFC/NIST/random roundtrip tests and explicitly states positive Turnkey-signed bundle tests are not present.

codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r3-20260523.md:17389:./codex-crypto-reviews/Encoding.cs-r1-20260523.md:308:src/turnkey-sdk-csharp.csproj:12:    <Description>Unofficial / community-maintained Turnkey SDK for .NET by Kyuzan Inc. Not affiliated with Turnkey, Inc. 1:1 logical port of @turnkey/crypto v2.8.8, @turnkey/http v3.16.0, @turnkey/api-key-stamper v0.5.0, and @turnkey/encoding v0.6.0 (peak-pinned versions). Provides P-256 ECDSA / HPKE / HKDF / API key stamping primitives for .NET. BouncyCastle 2.5.0 is used only for primitives; HPKE / HKDF / bundle parsing are direct ports of the upstream Turnkey TypeScript logic.</Description>

codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r3-20260523.md:19908:| `GetPublicKey(byte[])` (`src/Crypto.cs:344`) | `ts-source/crypto.ts:80` | REVIEWED | Invalid-key validation divergence in E. |
codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r3-20260523.md:19935:- C# `byte[]` plus `Array.Copy` replaces `Uint8Array.slice/set/spread`; structural byte movement only.
codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r3-20260523.md:19941:- Sync C# methods replace async TS wrappers where no C# async operation is needed; output bytes unchanged.
codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r3-20260523.md:19943:- Ed25519 seed-to-public derivation uses BouncyCastle for the SOLANA export branch; intended same 32-byte public key.
codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r3-20260523.md:19954:| `src/Crypto.cs:344`, `src/Crypto.cs:364` | `ts-source/crypto.ts:80` | C# accepts non-32-byte private key inputs and does not explicitly reject scalar `0` / `>= n`; noble `p256.getPublicKey` validates exact key size and scalar range. | Add a shared P-256 private-key validator: exactly 32 bytes and scalar in `[1, n - 1]`. |
codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r3-20260523.md:19958:- `tests/Fixtures/crypto/turnkey-crypto-vectors.json` is pinned to `@turnkey/crypto@2.8.8` and the same tarball SHA above.
codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r3-20260523.md:19959:- Its credential bundle vector cites `ts-source/__tests__/crypto-test.ts:179-184`; `tests/CryptoTests.cs:483-495` asserts the same decrypted bytes.
codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r3-20260523.md:19966:- I could not execute the pinned Node package directly because the extracted tarball does not include its dependencies, so fixture validation here is source-level against the pinned upstream snapshot.
codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r3-20260523.md:19986:| `GetPublicKey(byte[])` (`src/Crypto.cs:344`) | `ts-source/crypto.ts:80` | REVIEWED | Invalid-key validation divergence in E. |
codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r3-20260523.md:20013:- C# `byte[]` plus `Array.Copy` replaces `Uint8Array.slice/set/spread`; structural byte movement only.
codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r3-20260523.md:20019:- Sync C# methods replace async TS wrappers where no C# async operation is needed; output bytes unchanged.
codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r3-20260523.md:20021:- Ed25519 seed-to-public derivation uses BouncyCastle for the SOLANA export branch; intended same 32-byte public key.
codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r3-20260523.md:20032:| `src/Crypto.cs:344`, `src/Crypto.cs:364` | `ts-source/crypto.ts:80` | C# accepts non-32-byte private key inputs and does not explicitly reject scalar `0` / `>= n`; noble `p256.getPublicKey` validates exact key size and scalar range. | Add a shared P-256 private-key validator: exactly 32 bytes and scalar in `[1, n - 1]`. |
codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r3-20260523.md:20036:- `tests/Fixtures/crypto/turnkey-crypto-vectors.json` is pinned to `@turnkey/crypto@2.8.8` and the same tarball SHA above.
codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r3-20260523.md:20037:- Its credential bundle vector cites `ts-source/__tests__/crypto-test.ts:179-184`; `tests/CryptoTests.cs:483-495` asserts the same decrypted bytes.

codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r3-20260523.md:20727:- `byte[]`/`Array.Copy` replaces `Uint8Array.slice/set/spread`; same byte ordering and lengths.
codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r3-20260523.md:20728:- C# overloads replace TS union inputs; same accepted hex/byte semantics.
codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r3-20260523.md:20731:- Manual HKDF-HMAC-SHA256 replaces `@noble/hashes/hkdf`; same RFC 5869 extract/expand bytes.
codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r3-20260523.md:20755:- `tests/Fixtures/crypto/turnkey-crypto-vectors.json` is pinned to `@turnkey/crypto@2.8.8` and the same tarball SHA.
codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r3-20260523.md:20756:- Its `decryptCredentialBundle` vector comes from `ts-source/__tests__/crypto-test.ts:179-184`; `tests/CryptoTests.cs:525-537` asserts the same decrypted hex bytes.

codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r3-20260523.md:20764:- Fixture validation was source-level against the pinned upstream snapshot, not a fresh Node execution of the package.

/bin/zsh -lc 'nl -ba tests/Fixtures/README.md' in /Users/takeshi/Kyuzan/src/turnkey-sdk-csharp
 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	# Test fixtures
     2	
     3	Each subdirectory groups fixtures for one production file, plus a
     4	provenance note explaining where the bytes came from.
     5	
     6	## Provenance levels
     7	
     8	- **upstream-test-vectors** — directly copied from the upstream
     9	  `__tests__/` directory inside `codex-crypto-reviews/upstream-snapshots/`.
    10	- **rfc** — standardized test vectors (RFC 5869, RFC 9180, etc.).
    11	- **nist** — NIST CAVP P-256 vectors.
    12	- **turnkey-sample** — sample bundles published by Turnkey in their
    13	  developer docs (de-identified).
    14	- **node-generated** — bytes generated by running the pinned upstream Node
    15	  package inside `tests/Fixtures/Generators/`. The generator script,
    16	  Node version, and output hash are recorded so reviewers can re-run.
    17	
    18	## Layout
    19	
    20	```
    21	Fixtures/
    22	├── encoding/
    23	│   └── turnkey-encoding-vectors.json       — upstream-test-vectors
    24	├── crypto/                                  — (added with Crypto.cs port)
    25	├── api-key-stamper/                         — (added with ApiKeyStamper.cs port)
    26	└── http/                                    — (added with Http.cs port)
    27	```
    28	
    29	## Re-generating
    30	
    31	Fixtures sourced from upstream test files are stable as long as
    32	[`codex-crypto-reviews/upstream-snapshots/`](../../codex-crypto-reviews/upstream-snapshots/)
    33	is not bumped. If a pin is bumped, re-extract the upstream `__tests__/`
    34	into the matching fixture file and update its sha256 in
    35	`tarball-checksums.txt` next to the new tarball.

     7	  privateKey: string;
     8	  publicKey: string;
     9	  pemPublicKey: string;
    10	}> {
    11	  const privateKey = (
    12	    await fs.promises.readFile(
    13	      path.resolve(FIXTURES_DIR, "api-key.private"),
    14	      "utf-8",
    15	    )
    16	  ).trim();
    17	
    18	  // These two formats represent the same public key
    19	  const publicKey = (
    20	    await fs.promises.readFile(
    21	      path.resolve(FIXTURES_DIR, "api-key.public"),
    22	      "utf-8",
    23	    )
    24	  ).trim();
    25	  const pemPublicKey = (
    26	    await fs.promises.readFile(
    27	      path.resolve(FIXTURES_DIR, "api-key.public.pem"),
    28	      "utf-8",
    29	    )
    30	  ).trim();
    31	
    32	  return {
    33	    privateKey,
    34	    publicKey,
    35	    pemPublicKey,
    36	  };
    37	}

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	'use strict';
     2	
     3	var p256 = require('@noble/curves/p256');
     4	var sha256Uint8array = require('sha256-uint8array');
     5	var encoding = require('@turnkey/encoding');
     6	
     7	const signWithApiKey = async (input) => {
     8	    const publicKey = p256.p256.getPublicKey(input.privateKey, true);
     9	    // Public key in the usual 02 or 03 + 64 hex digits
    10	    const publicKeyString = encoding.uint8ArrayToHexString(publicKey);
    11	    if (publicKeyString != input.publicKey) {
    12	        throw new Error(`Bad API key. Expected to get public key ${input.publicKey}, got ${publicKeyString}`);
    13	    }
    14	    const hash = sha256Uint8array.createHash().update(input.content).digest();
    15	    const signature = p256.p256.sign(hash, input.privateKey);
    16	    return signature.toDERHex();
    17	};
    18	
    19	exports.signWithApiKey = signWithApiKey;
    20	//# sourceMappingURL=purejs.js.map

    88	        /// Upstream: <c>hex.ts uint8ArrayToHexString</c>.

    90	        public static string Uint8ArrayToHexString(byte[] input)
    91	        {
    92	            if (input == null)
    93	            {
    94	                throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(input));
    95	            }
    96	
    97	            // Upstream uses Array.prototype.reduce starting at "". For an empty

   134	            // Upstream uses `if (!length)`, which treats `0` as falsy/omitted.

   136	            if (!length.HasValue || length.Value == 0)

   149	            Array.Copy(buffer, 0, paddedBuffer, length.Value - buffer.Length, buffer.Length);
   150	            return paddedBuffer;

   348	        /// 0-255 byte range.
   349	        /// </remarks>
   350	        public static string StringToBase64UrlString(string input)

   353	            {

   355	            }

**A. Source Pin Acknowledgement**

- Upstream package: `@turnkey/api-key-stamper`
- Version: `0.5.0`
- Tarball SHA-256: `962a2d22c7c40240f05be98533769b37ab7dad7dbb5abec762c41007233d02bd`
- C# file git SHA: `faacaec4bae05e068a0d4ff71954106b9d199d64`

**B. Method Coverage Table**

| C# method (file:line) | Upstream TS function (path:line) | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `ApiKeyStamper.ApiKeyStamper(string, string)` (`src/ApiKeyStamper.cs:82`) | `ApiKeyStamper.constructor` (`ts-source/index.ts:78`) | REVIEWED | Both store public/private key material without constructor-time validation. |
| `Stamp(string)` (`src/ApiKeyStamper.cs:123`) | `ApiKeyStamper.stamp` (`ts-source/index.ts:84`) | REVIEWED | Reviewed header name, stamp object shape, JSON property order, and base64url wrapping. |
| `SignWithApiKey(string)` (`src/ApiKeyStamper.cs:153`) | `signWithApiKey` purejs path (`ts-source/purejs.ts:5`) and universal dispatcher (`ts-source/index.ts:48`) | REVIEWED | Reviewed P-256 public-key derivation, SHA-256, ECDSA, DER hex output, and runtime-selection implications. |
| `EncodeDerSignatureHex(BigInteger, BigInteger)` (`src/ApiKeyStamper.cs:221`) | `signature.toDERHex()` (`ts-source/purejs.ts:23`) | REVIEWED | Reviewed ASN.1 DER integer padding and sequence encoding. |

**C. Intentional Adaptations**

- TS config object becomes C# positional constructor parameters; same two stored values.
- TS `async` methods become synchronous C# methods; crypto work is local and produces the same intended return payload shape.
- Dynamic TS import shape is replaced by direct C# implementation; this is structural only if the parity target is explicitly `purejs`.
- `JSON.stringify` is replaced by source-generated `System.Text.Json` with `[JsonPropertyName]`; property order is preserved by declaration order.
- `Uint8Array` operations become `byte[]` and `BigInteger`; same intended byte ordering and hex encoding.
- Noble/Node crypto APIs are replaced by BouncyCastle primitives; this is structural only where algorithm options match exactly. The low-S option does not currently match; see E.

**D. D17 Enforcement Check**

N/A (not Crypto.cs).

**E. Logic Divergence Findings**

| C# file:line | TS upstream path:line | Semantic diff | Suggested fix |
|---|---|---|---|
| `src/ApiKeyStamper.cs:45-53`, `src/ApiKeyStamper.cs:153` | `ts-source/index.ts:31-64`, `ts-source/nodecrypto.ts:21-25`, `ts-source/purejs.ts:5-23` | C# always implements the `purejs` path. Upstream `ApiKeyStamper` without `runtimeOverride` dispatches by environment; in Node it uses `nodecrypto.ts`, producing OpenSSL ECDSA signatures rather than the deterministic purejs path. This is observable in signature bytes and some error timing. | Either implement/document a package-default runtime parity mode, or make the review/fixtures explicitly target `runtimeOverride: "purejs"` and avoid claiming default upstream parity. |
| `src/ApiKeyStamper.cs:199-213` | `ts-source/purejs.ts:22-23`, `package/package.json:51` | C# forcibly normalizes ECDSA `s` to low-S. Upstream `purejs.ts` calls `p256.sign(hash, privateKey)` with no options. The pinned package depends on `@noble/curves` `^1.3.0`; inspected compatible Noble P-256 code defaults `lowS: false`, so high-S DER signatures can be emitted. Example for `"hello from TKHQ!"`: Noble-compatible purejs emitted high-S DER `304602...b03f...`, while C# would normalize to low-S DER `304502...4fc0...`. | Remove the low-S clamp for upstream purejs byte parity, or generate/compare fixtures against an explicitly modified upstream call using `{ lowS: true }`. |

**F. Fixture Comparison Gate**

Not passed.

- `tests/Fixtures/api-key-stamper/turnkey-api-key-stamper-vectors.json` does exercise this file and is sourced from the pinned upstream package/test metadata (`@turnkey/api-key-stamper@0.5.0`, same tarball SHA).
- The C# tests do not assert the same bytes Node would produce. `tests/ApiKeyStamperTests.cs:72-108` asserts header name, decoded JSON key order, public key, scheme, and signature verification, but not exact `stampHeaderValue` or exact DER signature bytes.
- The fixture itself says exact Node-generated byte snapshots were not used, and upstream `stamp-test.ts:29-30` also avoids snapshots because signatures are not deterministic across runtimes.
- `tests/Fixtures/encoding/...` and `tests/Fixtures/crypto/...` do not exercise `src/ApiKeyStamper.cs`.

**G. Unresolved Assumptions**

- I could not run `dotnet test`; MSBuild failed under the read-only sandbox with a named-pipe `Permission denied` error.
- I did not run the pinned upstream package end-to-end with its own installed dependency tree; the snapshot has source/dist but no local `node_modules`.
- The exact Noble version resolved at upstream publish time is not locked in the snapshot; `package.json` only pins `@noble/curves` as `^1.3.0`.

Round 3 does not meet the stated PASS criterion: B and D are covered, but E has divergences and F is not “all fixtures match.”
